sanskrit:
अर्जुन उवाच।
ये शास्त्रविधिमुत्सृज्य यजन्ते श्रद्धयान्विताः।
तेषां निष्ठा तु का कृष्ण सत्त्वमाहो रजस्तमः॥
transliteration:
arjuna uvāca
ye śāstra-vidhim utsṛjya yajante śraddhayānvitāḥ
yeṣāṃ niṣṭhā tu kā kṛṣṇa sattvam āho rajas tamaḥ
literal_meaning: Arjuna said: Those who, abandoning the scriptural ordinance, worship with faith—what is their standing, O Kṛṣṇa: is it sattva, rajas, or tamas?
contextual_meaning: Arjuna asks about people who act from sincere inner conviction yet bypass formal rules: are they psychologically in clarity, agitation, or darkness?
psychological_state: Cognitive-dissonance: valuing both personal faith and external validation, seeking a developmental map for non-conformist spirituality.
trigger_scenario: A client who quit organized religion but still meditates daily asks, 'Am I just fooling myself or is this real growth?'
anchor_text: Faithful hearts transcend rules—yet clarity, passion, or delusion shapes their path.
